Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ernest Der and Ryan Meckfessel for Barbara Borgfeldt Krieg.

Mr. Meckfessel stated that Mr. Skootsky would not be appearing today. The Court stated that before it today are two petitions: the Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustees and Petition for Allowance of Fees; and the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and Modification of Trust in Accordance with its Terms. Mr. Der stated that the objections with regard to the thirteenth account are withdrawn. Mr. Meckfessel addressed CCP 664.6 stating that it was included and is attached to the petition. Brief discussions with regard to CCP 664.6 and the Court retaining jursidiction are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court took both of the petitions: the Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustees and Petition for Allowance of Fees (to be "allowed" per stipulation); and the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and Modification of Trust in Accordance with its Terms under submission. The Court noted that the proposed order for the petition for approval of the settlement agreement would be modified with regard to CCP 664.6 and would reflect language that states that the Court "agrees to retain jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement to the extent that the settlement is enforceable", etc.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Continued To Oct-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Barbara Borgfeldt Krieg and Dawn Maroney (Co-Trustees).
Greg Menzel and Douglas Voorsanger for Roxanne Borgfeldt (Objector).

Mr. Skootksy stated that they had mediation, that they did not settle, that they are still having settlement discussion and that they have agreed to continue the matter to October 29th. Mr. Skootsky also stated that he just came on a month ago, that in the interim they will have further settlement discussion and that the likelihood is the settlement agreement will need Court approval. The Court noted that the request for the Court to order an accounting from the corporation is problematic and that the parties need to keep that in mind. The Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to October 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan West and Kenneth Mitchell for and with Dawn Maroney.
Greg Menzel for Roxanne Borgfeldt (Objector).
Valerie Borgfeldt in Propria Persona (Remainder Beneficiary).

Mr. West stated that a reply to the objections was filed. The Court noted that it was filed late. Mr. Menzel stated that he has not had a chance to review the reply thoroughly, that he wasn't served, that he is not prepared to respond to the reply, asked the Court to send the parties to mediation and stated that there is over $20,000,000.00 in the Trust. Mr. West stated that mediation makes sense, that they are here for an accounting and that they are not here to dictate to the Trustees how to administer the trust. Mr. Menzel stated that the corporation has about $10,000,000.00 in assets, that corporation shares are owned by the Trust and asked why the Trustees are not doing anything to reduce tax burdens down the road. Mr. West stated that they intend on valuating the shares. The Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustees, etc. is continued to March 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages the parties to communicate and cooperate, and reminds Counsel that courtesy copies are to be provided to the Court.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Continued To Nov-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Julius Kahn and Alan West for Barbara Krieg.
Greg Menzel for Roxanne Borgfelt (Beneficiary).

Mr. Kahn informed the Court that he is withdrawing for personal reasons and that Mr. West will be taking over. Mr. West stated that he associated in several months ago and that he will be filing a reply by the end of next week. Mr. Kahn also stated that Valerie Borgfelt has always been noticed at Roxanne's address even when she moved to San Carlos, spoke of Valerie's condition, stated that Roxanne has always accepted service and that nobody has objected to providing that service to Roxanne. Mr. Menzel stated Roxanne is estranged from her Sister, that he provided a current address for Valerie, that he just received the corporate tax returns and requested a continuance until November. Mr. Kahn stated that he does not require any further notices. The Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustees, etc. is continued to November 13, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that time records are required to substantiate their request for guideline fees.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Continued To Aug-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Julius Kahn for and with Dawn Maroney and for Barbara Borgfeldt (Co-Trustees).

Mr. Kahn stated that he filed a supplement and that he did not have to give 30-day notice. The Court informed Counsel that notice is required because Judge Cheng required that the petition be amended and because he is asking for two material changes (1031 exchanges and an increase in fees). The Court also stated that it does not see how the Trust can convey real property when the Corporation holds title. The Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to August 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Continued To Jun-28-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Julius Kahn III for and with Dawn Maroney (Co-Trustee).

Mr. Kahn stated that this is the 13th account, that they have never valuated the stock, that there is only properties and cash, and that he is at a loss as to why he has to valuate the stock. Mr. Kahn also stated that they are dealing with six remainder Beneficiaries, that they are not a close family, that they are looking at doing 1031 exchanges, that each of the beneficiaries may want something different, that this will give them the option of either to sell of keep their interests when the time is appropriate and that the properties are under constant management. In addition, Mr. Kahn stated that he can amend the petition, that they have provided all documents requested under the first set of Examiner's notes, that the Trustee's should have the authority to do the exchanges, that the Trustees' judgment have been good in the past, that each beneficiary could deal with their inheritance as they see fit, that he does not recommend the Trustees sell the property because there would be a huge capital gain, that they would leave it up to the individuals to do that and that this would put the beneficiaries in a position where they would have options. The Thirteenth Account and Report of Successor Trustees, etc. is continued to June 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to amend the petition to incorporate the appropriate relief in the caption and in the prayer.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #12, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julius Kahn for Barbara Borgfeldt Krieg.

Mr. Kahn apologized, stated that he had been frustrated with the examiners notes, that 30 days notice was given, that the notice was filed 2 days later, that Ms. Krieg is the last income beneficiary, that this is their 12th accounting and is in the same format they have always used, that the fees are reasonable, that his office is in San Ramon and the trustee is in Danville and it takes 2 1/2 hours to get here, that the examiner cited a rule of court for local attorneys but he is not a local attorney, that his clients have never taken fees from the principal only from the income and that the only time they used principal funds was for the 1040 exchange. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
